webcam.c(3) Library Functions Manual webcam.c(3)

NAME

webcam.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t webcam_init (unsigned short int webcam_index)
image_t * webcam_read (void)
void webcam_destroy (void)
void webcam_flush (void)
void webcam_print_init_error_help (asciichat_error_t error_code)
asciichat_error_t webcam_init_context (webcam_context_t **ctx, unsigned short int device_index)
void webcam_cleanup_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
void webcam_flush_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
image_t * webcam_read_context (webcam_context_t *ctx)
asciichat_error_t webcam_get_dimensions (webcam_context_t *ctx, int *width, int *height)
asciichat_error_t webcam_list_devices (webcam_device_info_t **out_devices, unsigned int *out_count)
void webcam_free_device_list (webcam_device_info_t *devices)

Function Documentation

void webcam_cleanup_context (webcam_context_t * ctx)

Definition at line 253 of file video/webcam/webcam.c.

253                                                    {
254   (void)ctx;
255   log_warn("Webcam cleanup called on unsupported platform");
256 }




Referenced by media_source_destroy(), and
    webcam_destroy().






void
  webcam_destroy (void )


Definition at line 137 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


137                           {
138   // Free cached webcam frame if it was allocated (from test pattern or real webcam)
139   if (cached_webcam_frame) {
140     image_destroy(cached_webcam_frame);
141     cached_webcam_frame = NULL;
142   }
143 
144   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
145     log_debug("Test pattern mode - webcam context cleanup skipped");
146     return;
147   }
148 
149   if (global_webcam_ctx) {
150     NAMED_UNREGISTER(global_webcam_ctx);
151     webcam_cleanup_context(global_webcam_ctx);
152     global_webcam_ctx = NULL;
153     // log_info("Webcam resources released");
154   } else {
155     log_dev("Webcam was not opened, nothing to release");
156   }
157 }






References image_destroy(), and
    webcam_cleanup_context().


Referenced by ascii_read_destroy().






void
  webcam_flush (void )


Definition at line 159 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


159                         {
160   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
161     return; // Test pattern doesn't need flushing
162   }
163 
164   if (global_webcam_ctx) {
165     webcam_flush_context(global_webcam_ctx);
166   }
167 }

Referenced by webcam_init().






asciichat_error_t
  webcam_init (unsigned short int webcam_index)


Definition at line 21 of file
  video/webcam/webcam.c.


21                                                                {
22   // Check if test pattern mode is enabled
23   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
24     log_info("Test pattern mode enabled - not opening real webcam");
25     log_info("Test pattern resolution: 320x240");
26     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
27   }
28 
29 #ifdef __linux__
30   log_info("Initializing webcam with V4L2 (Linux)");
31   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using V4L2 (Linux)...", webcam_index);
32 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
33   log_info("Initializing webcam with AVFoundation (macOS)");
34   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using AVFoundation (macOS)...", webcam_index);
35 #elif defined(_WIN32)
36   log_info("Initializing webcam with Media Foundation (Windows)");
37   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using Media Foundation (Windows)...", webcam_index);
38 #else
39   log_info("Initializing webcam with Unknown platform");
40   log_info("Attempting to open webcam with index %d using Unknown platform...", webcam_index);
41 #endif
42 
43   asciichat_error_t result = webcam_init_context(&global_webcam_ctx, webcam_index);
44   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
45     SET_ERRNO(result, "Failed to connect to webcam (error code: %d)", result);
46     return result;
47   }
48 
49   /* Register global webcam context with named registry */
50   char webcam_name[32];
51   SAFE_SNPRINTF(webcam_name, sizeof(webcam_name), "webcam:%u", webcam_index);
52   NAMED_REGISTER(global_webcam_ctx, webcam_name, "webcam", "0x%tx", NULL);
53 
54   // Get image dimensions
55   int width, height;
56   if (webcam_get_dimensions(global_webcam_ctx, &width, &height) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     log_info("Webcam opened successfully! Resolution: %dx%d", width, height);
58   } else {
59     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam opened but failed to get dimensions");
60   }
61 
62   return result;
63 }






References webcam_get_dimensions(), and
    webcam_init_context().

asciichat_error_t
  webcam_list_devices (webcam_device_info_t ** out_devices, unsigned int *
  out_count)


Definition at line 276 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


276                                                                                                    {
277   if (out_devices)
278     *out_devices = NULL;
279   if (out_count)
280     *out_count = 0;
281   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam device enumeration not supported on this platform");
282 }










void
  webcam_print_init_error_help (asciichat_error_t error_code)


Definition at line 169 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


169                                                                 {
170   // Platform-specific error messages and troubleshooting help
171 #ifdef __linux__
172   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
173 
174   if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM) {
175     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam initialization failed on Linux.\n\n");
176     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Common solutions:\n");
177     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  1. Check if a camera is connected:\n");
178     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       ls /dev/video*\n\n");
179     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  2. If no camera is available, use test pattern mode:\n");
180     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n\n");
181     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  3. Install V4L2 drivers if needed:\n");
182     safe_fprintf(stderr, "       sudo apt-get install v4l-utils\n");
183   } else if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION) {
184     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Camera permission denied.\n\n");
185     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Fix permissions with:\n");
186     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  sudo usermod -a -G video $USER\n");
187     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Then log out and log back in for changes to take effect.\n");
188   } else if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE) {
189     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Camera is already in use by another application.\n\n");
190     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Try closing other camera apps or use test pattern mode:\n");
191     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
192   } else {
193     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam error on Linux.\n\n");
194     safe_fprintf(stderr, "General troubleshooting:\n");
195     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Check camera: ls /dev/video*\n");
196     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Check permissions: groups | grep video\n");
197     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Use test pattern: ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
198   }
199   (void)fflush(stderr);
200 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
201   (void)error_code;
202   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
203   safe_fprintf(stderr, "On macOS, you may need to grant camera permissions:\n");
204   safe_fprintf(stderr,
205                "* Say \"yes\" to the popup about system camera access that you see when running this program for the "
206                "first time.\n");
207   safe_fprintf(
208       stderr, "* If you said \"no\" to the popup, go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy > Camera.\n");
209   safe_fprintf(stderr,
210                "   Now flip the switch next to your terminal application in that privacy list to allow ascii-chat to "
211                "access your camera.\n");
212   safe_fprintf(stderr, "   Then just run this program again.\n");
213   (void)fflush(stderr);
214 #elif defined(_WIN32)
215   if (error_code == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE) {
216     // Device is in use by another application - this is a fatal error on Windows
217     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
218     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Webcam is already in use by another application.\n");
219     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Windows allows only one application to access the webcam at a time.\n");
220     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
221     safe_fprintf(stderr, "To use ascii-chat with multiple clients, try these alternatives:\n");
222     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  --test-pattern    Generate a colorful test pattern instead of using webcam\n");
223     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  --file VIDEO.mp4  Use a video file as input (to be implemented)\n");
224     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
225     safe_fprintf(stderr, "Example: ascii-chat client --test-pattern\n");
226     (void)fflush(stderr);
227   } else {
228     // Other webcam errors - general failure
229     safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
230     safe_fprintf(stderr, "On Windows, this might be because:\n");
231     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Camera permissions are not granted\n");
232     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* Camera driver issues\n");
233     safe_fprintf(stderr, "* No webcam device found\n");
234     (void)fflush(stderr);
235   }
236 #else
237   // Unknown platform
238   (void)error_code;
239   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\nWebcam initialization failed on unsupported platform.\n");
240   (void)fflush(stderr);
241 #endif
242 }






References error_code, and safe_fprintf().


Referenced by client_main().






image_t
  * webcam_read (void )


Definition at line 65 of file
  video/webcam/webcam.c.


65                            {
66   // Check if test pattern mode is enabled
67   if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
68     // Allocate cached frame once (like FFmpeg's current_image)
69     // Reuse same buffer for each call to avoid repeated allocations
70     if (!cached_webcam_frame) {
71       cached_webcam_frame = image_new(320, 240);
72       if (!cached_webcam_frame) {
73         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate test pattern frame");
74         return NULL;
75       }
76     }
77 
78     // Generate animated test pattern each frame
79     // Animation is based on frame counter, respects FPS setting
80     unsigned int animation_phase = frame_counter / 2; // Slow down animation
81     frame_counter++;
82 
83     for (int y = 0; y < cached_webcam_frame->h; y++) {
84       for (int x = 0; x < cached_webcam_frame->w; x++) {
85         rgb_pixel_t *pixel = &cached_webcam_frame->pixels[y * cached_webcam_frame->w + x];
86 
87         // Animated color bars that shift based on frame counter
88         int animated_x = (x + animation_phase) % cached_webcam_frame->w;
89         int grid_x = animated_x / 40;
90 
91         // Base pattern: color bars that animate horizontally
92         switch (grid_x % 3) {
93         case 0: // Red
94           pixel->r = 255;
95           pixel->g = 0;
96           pixel->b = 0;
97           break;
98         case 1: // Green
99           pixel->r = 0;
100           pixel->g = 255;
101           pixel->b = 0;
102           break;
103         case 2: // Blue
104         default:
105           pixel->r = 0;
106           pixel->g = 0;
107           pixel->b = 255;
108           break;
109         }
110 
111         // Add animated grid lines
112         if (animated_x % 40 == 0 || y % 30 == 0) {
113           pixel->r = 0;
114           pixel->g = 0;
115           pixel->b = 0;
116         }
117       }
118     }
119 
120     return cached_webcam_frame;
121   }
122 
123   if (!global_webcam_ctx) {
124     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam not initialized - global_webcam_ctx is NULL");
125     return NULL;
126   }
127 
128   image_t *frame = webcam_read_context(global_webcam_ctx);
129 
130   if (!frame) {
131     return NULL;
132   }
133 
134   return frame;
135 }






References frame, image_new(), and
    webcam_read_context().

  * webcam_read_context (webcam_context_t * ctx)


Definition at line 263 of file
    video/webcam/webcam.c.


263                                                     {
264   (void)ctx;
265   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_WEBCAM, "Webcam read not supported on this platform");
266   return NULL;
267 }

    webcam_read().
